# Code Review — 2026-06-10

Scope: full local repo at HEAD `c6ff747`. Evidence base: README/pyproject, CI workflows (`ci.yml`, `publish.yml`), crypto & secret paths (`core/identity.py`, `core/db.py`, `adapters/email/oauth2.py`), LLM triage (`adapters/triage/scrubber.py`, `classifier.py`), registry loader (`registry/loader.py`), CLI surface (`cli/app.py`, `cli/console.py`).

- [ ] **[Security] LLM PII consent check fails open on unreadable consent file**
  - **Status quo:** `llm_consent_granted()` returns `True` whenever the consent file exists but raises `JSONDecodeError` **or `OSError`** (`src/symeraseme/adapters/triage/scrubber.py:125`). The intent is backward compatibility with legacy empty touch files, but the broad except means a corrupted file or a permission error silently grants consent to send (scrubbed) email content to an external LLM.
  - **Proposed solution:** Fail closed by default. Treat only the documented legacy case (file exists and `stat().st_size == 0`) as granted; on `JSONDecodeError`/`OSError` log a warning and return `False`, instructing the user to re-run consent granting.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / high impact

- [ ] **[Security] Release-publishing workflow uses mutable action refs with `id-token: write`**
  - **Status quo:** `publish.yml` grants `id-token: write` (PyPI trusted publishing) and runs `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` — a mutable branch ref — plus tag-pinned `actions/checkout@v6`, `setup-python@v6`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v7` (`.github/workflows/publish.yml:10,21,39`). A compromised upstream tag/branch could exfiltrate the OIDC token or tamper with the published wheel. Same tag-pinning applies in `ci.yml`.
  - **Proposed solution:** Pin all third-party actions to full commit SHAs (with a `# vX.Y.Z` comment). Dependabot already manages the workflows group, so SHA bumps stay automated.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / high impact

- [ ] **[Security] OAuth2 CSRF state file written with default permissions**
  - **Status quo:** `_store_oauth2_state()` writes `~/.local/share/symeraseme/oauth2_state.json` via plain `path.open("w")` and creates the parent dir without a mode (`src/symeraseme/adapters/email/oauth2.py:58-72`). Unlike the consent file (0600 via `os.open`) and the identity profile (0700/0600), pending OAuth state is readable by other local users, weakening the CSRF protection on the loopback callback server.
  - **Proposed solution:** Create the file with `os.open(..., O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0o600)` (mirroring `grant_llm_consent` in `scrubber.py:138`) and pass `mode=0o700` to the parent `mkdir`.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / medium impact

- [ ] **[UX/DX] No top-level `--version` flag**
  - **Status quo:** Version info is only available as the `version` subcommand (`src/symeraseme/cli/commands/inspection_commands.py:42`); `symeraseme --version` — the universal CLI convention and what scripts/agents try first — errors out. The root callback (`src/symeraseme/cli/app.py:78`) has no version option.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add an eager `--version` option to the root callback that prints `Symaira EraseMe v{__version__}` and exits 0, delegating to the same data as the existing subcommand.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / medium impact

- [ ] **[UX/DX] Default log level INFO leaks internal log lines into every command run**
  - **Status quo:** The root callback configures `logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)` unconditionally when `--verbose` is off (`src/symeraseme/cli/app.py:84-88`). Any `logger.info` in core modules (e.g. DB migration notices in `core/db.py:137`, consent logging in `scrubber.py:141`) prints raw `INFO:module:...` lines alongside the rich-formatted output, which looks broken and adds stderr noise for scripted callers.
  - **Proposed solution:** Default to `logging.WARNING`; map `-v` to `INFO` and `-vv` (or `--debug`) to `DEBUG`. User-facing notices that matter should go through `print_info`/`print_warning` in `cli/console.py`.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / medium impact

- [ ] **[Architecture] Persistent broker cache goes stale when a YAML in a subdirectory is edited**
  - **Status quo:** `_broker_cache_key()` derives the cache key from the mtime/size of the top-level `registry/brokers` directory only (`src/symeraseme/registry/loader.py:218-222`). Broker files live in subdirectories (`registry/brokers/eu|uk|us/*.yaml`), and editing a file's content does not change the parent directory's mtime — so the persistent JSON cache (`_load_from_persistent_cache`, loader.py:368) keeps serving the old broker definition until `registry sync`/`clear_registry_cache()` happens to run. Contributors editing broker YAMLs see stale data with no error.
  - **Proposed solution:** Build the cache key from an aggregate over the tree — e.g. `max(st_mtime)` + file count across `rglob("*.yaml")` (one cheap `stat` pass, no parsing), or per-subdirectory mtimes. Keep `clear_registry_cache()` as the explicit escape hatch.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / high impact

- [ ] **[Architecture] Duplicated DB re-encrypt/cleanup logic in atexit and close paths**
  - **Status quo:** `_cleanup_temp_files()` (`src/symeraseme/core/db.py:208-230`) and `close_connection()` (`src/symeraseme/core/db.py:284-308`) contain two nearly identical ~20-line blocks (WAL checkpoint → `_encrypt_file` → unlink temp → WAL sibling cleanup). They have already drifted slightly (`tmp != orig` guard exists only in one) and any future fix to the encryption-at-close path must be applied twice.
  - **Proposed solution:** Extract a single `_reencrypt_and_remove_temp(orig, tmp)` helper used by both call sites, keeping the stricter `tmp != orig` guard.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / medium impact

- [ ] **[Performance] Encrypted DB is fully re-encrypted on every close, even for read-only commands**
  - **Status quo:** With `SYMERASEME_ENCRYPT_DB=1`, `close_connection()` unconditionally Fernet-encrypts the entire decrypted temp DB back over the original on every exit (`src/symeraseme/core/db.py:289-308`), and `connection_context` triggers this per command. Read-only commands like `plan status` or `requests list` pay a full read-encrypt-write of the whole database file each run.
  - **Proposed solution:** Skip re-encryption when nothing changed — capture SQLite's `PRAGMA data_version` (or a content hash of the temp file) after decrypt and compare at close; only re-encrypt on mismatch. Always still delete the temp file.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / high impact

- [ ] **[Performance] Cold registry load compiles the JSON Schema 1,277 times**
  - **Status quo:** `load_broker_yaml()` calls `jsonschema.validate(data, broker_schema())` per file (`src/symeraseme/registry/loader.py:72`). `jsonschema.validate` builds a fresh validator (schema compilation + ref resolution) on every call, so an unfiltered cold load of the ~1,277-broker registry recompiles the same schema ~1,277 times before Pydantic validates the same data again.
  - **Proposed solution:** Create one module-level `jsonschema.Draft202012Validator(broker_schema())` (lazily, alongside the existing `_BROKER_SCHEMA` cache) and call `validator.validate(data)` in `load_broker_yaml`.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / medium impact

## Conclusion

The codebase is in good shape — strict CLI result envelopes, keyring-backed AES-GCM identity storage, PKCE + state-validated OAuth2, and PII scrubbing before LLM calls are all already in place. The highest-leverage fixes are small: the **first recommended quick win is the LLM consent fail-open fix** (`scrubber.py:125`) — a three-line change that closes a real privacy hole — followed by SHA-pinning the publish workflow and the stale-broker-cache key fix.
